chapters.title: General Provisions Regarding Site and Building Requirements
Special Provisions for Certain Occupancy Building
Construction in Plots Part of Which Has Been Surrendered for Road Development
Safety Provisions for High Rise Buildings
Regularisation of Unpermitted Constructions and Deviations
Registration of Architects, Engineers Town Planners, etc.
Vigilance, Dangerous and Defective Works, Appeal, etc.

sections.title: Short title, applicability and commencement
paragraphs: (1) These rules called the Kerala Municipality Building Rules, 1999.
(2) They shall apply to all Municipalities in the State.
(3) They shall come into force at once.

sections.title: Definitions
paragraphs: In these rules unless the context otherwise requires:
(a) 'access' means the way to a plot or building;
(b) 'accessory building' means a building attached or detached to a building on a plot...
(cl) 'water closet' or 'WC' means a latrine with arrangement for flushing the pan with water...

sections.title: Applicability
paragraphs: These rules shall apply to newly erected buildings, altered portions, changes in occupancy, and land development.

sections.title: Essentiality of permit
paragraphs: No person shall develop or redevelop any parcel of land or construct any building without obtaining a permit from the Secretary.

sections.title: Application for development permit
paragraphs: Applications must be in Appendix AA with ownership documents, plans, and fees.
Special consultations are required for land within 100m of Defense properties or 30m of Railway boundaries.

sections.title: Application for building permit
paragraphs: Requires site plans (1:400 scale), building plans (1:100 scale), service plans, and parking plans.
Plans must be signed by a registered Architect/Engineer and the applicant.

sections.title: Extension and renewal of period of permits
paragraphs: Permits are valid for three years. Can be extended twice for three years each.
Renewal fee is 50% of the permit fee in force at the time of renewal.

sections.title: Deviation during construction
paragraphs: Minor deviations (shifting rooms/windows) don't require a new permit but must be shown in the completion plan.
Major deviations require a revised permit.

sections.title: Demolition or alteration of work unlawfully commenced
paragraphs: The Secretary can issue a provisional order to demolish or alter unauthorized work.
The owner must be given a notice to show cause before confirmation of the order.

chapter-4.title: General Provisions Regarding Site and Building Requirements

sections.title: General requirement regarding plot
paragraphs: (1) No land development or redevelopment shall be made or no building shall be constructed on any plot on any part of which there is deposited refuse, excreta or other offensive matter...

tables.title: Table 1: Clearance from Overhead Electric Lines

sections.title: Exterior and Interior open air spaces
paragraphs: Minimum front yard: 3m (for buildings up to 10m height).
Minimum rear yard: 2m.
Minimum side yards: 1.20m and 1.00m.

sections.title: Minimum distance between central line of street and building
paragraphs: Minimum 4.5 meters from central line and 3 meters from plot boundary.

sections.title: Occupancy of buildings
paragraphs: Classifications: Group A1 (Residential), A2 (Special Residential), B (Educational), C (Medical), D (Assembly), E (Office), F (Mercantile), G1 (Industrial), G2 (Small Industrial), H (Storage), I(1) & I(2) (Hazardous).

sections.title: Coverage and floor area ratio
paragraphs: (1) The maximum percentage of coverage permissible for each occupancy shall limit the maximum area at any floor of a building.

tables.title: Table 2: Coverage and Floor Area Ratio (F.A.R)

sections.title: Parking, loading and unloading spaces
paragraphs: Standard car parking space: 15 sq.m (5.5m x 2.7m).
Residential A1 requires 1 space per 8 units (up to 100 sq.m units) or 1 per unit (above 200 sq.m units).

sections.title: Mezzanine floor
paragraphs: (1) The floor area of mezzanine floor shall not exceed one third area of the main floor or room accommodating the mezzanine floor.
(2) The headroom measured from the surface of the floor to any point on the underside of the mezzanine floor shall not be less than 2.2 mts.

sections.title: Height of room
paragraphs: The height of room in a building other than residential occupancy shall be not less than 3.00 metres: Provided that in the case of air conditioned rooms it shall be not less than 2.4 metres.

sections.title: Water closet
paragraphs: Every building shall be provided with at least one water closet.

sections.title: Size of bathroom and latrine
paragraphs: (1) The area of bath-room shall not be less than 1.50 sq.m. with either side not less than 1.1m, carpet area of a latrine shall not be less than 1.10 square metres with one side not less than 1.0 metre: Provided that the area of combined bathroom and latrine shall be not less than 2.2 square metres with one side not less than 1.1 metres.
(2) The height of bathroom or latrine shall be not less than 2.20 metres.

sections.title: Staircases
paragraphs: (1) Any building having more than four floors including basement or sunken floors, shall have at least two staircases, one of which may be an external stairway: Provided that when the second staircase provided as external stairway conforms to the provisions of fire escape staircase, a separate fire escape stair need not be provided.
(2) The minimum width of stair shall be not less than 1.20 metres.
(3) The minimum width of tread shall be 30 cms.
(4) The height of riser shall not exceed 15 cms.
(5) The height of handrail shall be not less than 90 cms.
(6) The width of passages giving access to the staircase in any building shall not at any point, be less than the width of the stair.

sections.title: Ramps
paragraphs: Ramps if provided as a substitute for stairways shall be laid with a slope not exceeding 1 in 10 and such ramp shall comply with all requirements of a stairway and shall be surfaced with approved non-slippery materials.

sections.title: Corridor, verandahs and passageways
paragraphs: The clear width of any corridor, verandah or passageway in any building shall be not less than 1.0 metre at any point.

sections.title: Fire escape staircase
paragraphs: (1) Fire escape stair case shall be provided for every building of,-
(a) residential occupancies exceeding three storeys above ground level;
(b) occupancies other than residential exceeding two storeys above ground level.

(2) The width of fire escape staircase shall be not less than 75 cm, the width of fire escape stair tread shall be not less than 15 cm, the height of the fire escape stair riser shall not exceed 19 cms, and the number of riser shall not exceed 16 per flight of stairs.
(3) The height of handrail of a fire escape staircase shall not be less than 100 cms.
(4) Fire escape stair shall be constructed only in the exterior of the building and shall be connected directly to the ground.
(5) Fire escape stairs shall have a straight flight.
(6) Entrance to fire escape stair case shall be separated and removed from internal staircase.

sections.title: Travel distance to emergency staircase
paragraphs: (1) Every building meant for human occupancy shall be provided with emergency exit sufficient to permit safe escape of occupants in case of fire or whenever other emergency occurs.
(2) Emergency exits shall be located in such a way that the travel distance on each floor shall not exceed 30 metres for every occupant.

sections.title: Fire Protection requirements
paragraphs: All requirements in respect of fire protection shall be as in Part IV, Fire Protection in National Building Code of India, 1983 and amendment No. 3 under Fire Protection Annexure 11.

sections.title: Exit Width
paragraphs: (1) The unit of exit width used to measure the capacity of any exit shall be 50 cms., a clear width of 25 cms. shall be counted as an additional half unit and clear width less than 25 cms. shall not be counted for exit width.
(2) Occupants per unit exit width shall be as in Table 6.
(3) For determining the exit required, the occupant load shall be based on the actual number of occupants but not less than specified in Table 7.
(4) Exits shall be either horizontal or vertical type.
(5) An exit may be a doorway corridor or passageway to an internal or external staircase, ramps, etc. Lifts and escalators shall not be considered as exits.

tables.title: Table 6: Occupants per unit exit width
Table 7: Occupant Load
headers: Occupant Load (gross area in sq. meters per person)

sections.title: Doorways
paragraphs: (1) Every exit doorway shall open into an enclosed stairway or a horizontal exit providing protected means of egress.
(2) No exit doorway shall be less than 75 cms (residential) and 1.2 metres (other occupancies).

sections.title: Spiral stair fire escape
paragraphs: (1) Limited to low occupant load and buildings upto 9 metres in height.
(2) Diameter not less than 150 cm.

sections.title: Lift
paragraphs: (1) Buildings exceeding 3 storeys (hospitals) or 4 storeys (others) with plinth area > 2500 sq.m shall have one lift for every 2500 sq.m.
(3) At least one lift shall be a higher capacity lift that can carry a stretcher.

sections.title: Lighting and Ventilation
paragraphs: (1) Every habitable room shall have sufficient openings for light and air.
(2) No portion of a room shall be assumed lighted if it is more than 7.5 metres from an opening.
(6) Bathroom/latrine size of air shaft shall be per Table 8.

sections.title: Recreation space
paragraphs: (1) Residential apartments > 12 units shall provide recreation space.
(2) Not less than 7.5% of the total carpet area.

sections.title: Certain Buildings exempted
paragraphs: Family residential buildings with not more than three floors on plots not exceeding 7 cents are exempted from this chapter except for rule 42 and 44.

chapter-7.title: Special Provisions for Certain Occupancy Building

sections.title: Additional provisions to certain occupancy groups
paragraphs: The provisions in rules 23 to 51 shall apply to certain occupancy groups, subject to the modifications in rules 53 to 59.

sections.title: Residential occupancy
paragraphs: (1) Every floor of a flat, apartment... exceeding 150 sq. metres with capacity for more than 20 persons shall have at least 2 door ways.
(3) In buildings exceeding three storeys, a certificate of approval from the Director of Fire Force shall be obtained.

sections.title: Educational, Medical or Hospital and office or business occupancies
paragraphs: (1) Approval of the District Town Planner shall be obtained for usage of plot up to 0.5 hectares.
(4) Every hospital shall be provided with incinerator for disposal of hazardous and pathogenic wastes.

sections.title: Assembly occupancy
paragraphs: (3) The height of room in Assembly occupancy buildings shall be minimum 4 metres; Provided that in air conditioned auditoriums/halls it shall be minimum 3 metres.

sections.title: Mercantile or commercial occupancy
paragraphs: (2) The rear yard shall be not less than 1.5 metre depth in Group F- mercantile or commercial building.

sections.title: Industrial and small industrial occupancy
paragraphs: (4) All industrial buildings upto 10 metres height with area exceeding 200 sq. metres shall have specific open spaces.
(7) The minimum height of work room shall not be less than 3.6 metres.

sections.title: Storage and warehousing occupancy
paragraphs: (4) Buildings for handling food stuffs shall be effectively rat-proofed.

sections.title: Hazardous occupancy
paragraphs: (3) The minimum width of open yard all round the building shall be 3 metres for Group 1(1) and 7.5 metres for Group 1(2).

sections.title: Special provisions for construction in small plots
paragraphs: The provisions apply to residential or commercial occupancy in plots not exceeding 125 sq. metres.
Permit shall not be granted to the same person for multiple plots formed by division.

sections.title: Number of floors to be limited
paragraphs: The number of floors allowed shall be three.

sections.title: Conditions regarding set back
paragraphs: (1) Minimum distance from street: 2 metres (except NH/SH/District roads).
(2) Side yard: Min 90 cms on one side, 60 cms on other.
(3) Rear yard: Average 1 metre, minimum 0.50 metre.

sections.title: Certain provisions not to apply
paragraphs: Provisions regarding FAR, coverage, and access width shall not apply to buildings under this Chapter.

sections.title: Application and its disposal
paragraphs: Submission and disposal follow Chapter II procedures.

sections.title: Row buildings to be allowed on declared streets
paragraphs: The Secretary shall permit the construction or reconstruction of row buildings only on the sides or part of a side or sides of any street, where the Municipality, has by declaration published, decided to allow row buildings.

sections.title: Number of units that can be permitted
paragraphs: The Number of dwelling units in a row of buildings shall not exceed ten.
Note :- A row building with separate entry and exit and separated by common wall from other row buildings abutting it shall be deemed to be one unit for this purpose.

sections.title: Plot Area
paragraphs: The area of plot for one unit shall not exceed 85 Sq. metres.

sections.title: Distance from street, etc.
paragraphs: The minimum distance between the plot boundary abutting any street other than National Highways, State Highways, district roads and other roads notified by the Municipality and the building other than a compound wall or fence or outdoor display structure shall be 1.5 metres.

sections.title: Maximum floors
paragraphs: The Maximum number of floors permitted shall be two and a staircase room.

sections.title: Certain provisions not to apply
paragraphs: Provisions regarding F.A.R., Coverage, distance from the central line of road, access width, height restriction with regard to width of road and the yard abutting the road, dimension of building parts, light and ventilation and parking contained in these rules shall not be applicable to row buildings.

sections.title: Submission of applications etc.
paragraphs: (1) Application for permit and other matters required as per these rules may be submitted either jointly or individually.
(2) The provisions for submission and disposal of application, filing of completion certificate, extension and renewal of permit and similar matters shall be as in Chapter II.
(3) The application fee and permit fee shall be as in Schedule 1 and Schedule 11 respectively.

sections.title: Reconstruction, etc. of existing row buildings
paragraphs: Notwithstanding anything contained in these rules, in the case of existing row buildings, reconstruction, repair, alternation or addition, both horizontal and vertical shall be permitted irrespective of whether it is on the side of a street where row buildings are permitted or not, and irrespective of the plot area, use and set backs provided.

sections.title: Provision for construction under approved Schemes
paragraphs: Provisions in this chapter shall apply to construction or reconstruction or addition or alteration of any building financed or built by Government, Municipality, Housing Board, Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes Development Corporation, Fisherman Welfare Corporation, Matsyafed, Harijan Welfare Department, Social Welfare Department or any Government Department, Quasi Government Agency, or Housing Co-operative Societies under any Government approved Scheme for economically weaker sections.
The provisions is this Chapter shall also apply to buildings financed or built by any Municipality or Panchayat at any level to its beneficiaries.

sections.title: Area and floor restrictions
paragraphs: (1) Total plinth area of the building shall not exceed 50 Sq. mts.
(2) Number of floors shall be limited, to two and a staircase room.

sections.title: Setback provisions
paragraphs: (1) The minimum distance between the plot boundary abutting any street other than National Highways, State Highways, District Roads and other roads notified by the municipality and the building, other than a compound wall or fence or outdoor display structure, shall be minimum 1.50 metres.
(2) Front yard shall have minimum 1.00 metre width.

(3) In the case of individual developed plots requiring no lay out approval from the District Town Planner, there shall be average 60 cms open space on the sides and the rear: Provided that door shall not be allowed if that portion does not have minimum 1.00 metre open yard.

(4) In the case of buildings requiring layout approval of District Town Planner, the side yards and rear yard shall be provided as shown in the building layout: Provided that no portion of the building shall encroach, project or overhang beyond the plot boundaries.

sections.title: Certain provisions not to apply
paragraphs: Provisions regarding FAR, coverage, distance from central line of road, access width, height restriction with regard to width of road and the yard abutting the road dimensions with regard to building parts, light and ventilation shall not apply to building under this chapter.

sections.title: Layout approval to be obtained
paragraphs: Layout approval shall be obtained by the Department, Corporation, Board, Agency, Local Body or Society concerned from the District Town Planner and the Secretary in all cases where building permit is not required.

sections.title: Application for permit and its disposal
paragraphs: (1) Where the construction or reconstruction or addition or alteration is proposed to be done by individuals separately, an application in white paper, typed, or written in ink and affixed with necessary court fee stamp shall be submitted by the individual concerned to the Secretary, along with a site plan and document to prove his ownership. The number of floors and the area in each floor shall be specified in the application.
(2) Secretary shall, if convinced of the boundaries and bonafides of the ownership and that the building is in accordance with the rules in this Chapter, issue permit.

(3) Where the construction or reconstruction or addition or alteration is proposed to be done by the Department, Corporation, Board, Agency, Local body or Society themselves, layout for sub-division shall be obtained from the Secretary and no building permit is necessary.
(4) There shall be no application fee in the case of individual application and the permit fee in the case of individual application shall be as shown in Schedule-II.

chapter-11.title: Construction in Plots Part of Which Has Been Surrendered for Road Development

sections.title: Provisions to apply as modified for constructions in certain plots
paragraphs: Applies when part of a plot is surrendered free of cost for road widening/projects.

sections.title: Coverage and floor area ratio
paragraphs: (1) Max coverage: 80% for residential/mercantile, 60% for assembly/office.
(2) FAR area of surrendered land is also taken into account.

sections.title: Set back, height, etc.
paragraphs: (1) Minimum 3 metres from proposed road widening, or 1.5m with Special Committee approval.

sections.title: Special Committees
paragraphs: Government may constitute Special Committees to decide on permits under this chapter.

sections.title: Certain buildings and sheds exempted
paragraphs: Dog/cattle sheds for limited numbers and aviary for up to 20 hens are exempted.

sections.title: Temporary hut or shed
paragraphs: Secretary may grant permission for temporary huts/sheds for a specified period.

sections.title: Accessory building
paragraphs: (1) Bathrooms, toilets, storehouses... permitted in open space if limited to 15% of open space.
(3) Height limited to 2.5 mts.
(5) Minimum 1.00-meter distance from boundaries.

sections.title: Conversion of roof and shutters in existing building
paragraphs: Secretary may permit conversion of thatched roof with tile/asbestos.

sections.title: Certain provisions not to apply
paragraphs: Provisions regarding FAR, coverage, and setbacks shall not apply to works under this chapter.

sections.title: Prohibition of commencement of work
paragraphs: Construction of wall/fence over 1.5m (or 2m on some sides) requires permission.

sections.title: Submission and disposal of application
paragraphs: (5a) Wall/fence at street junction shall be sufficiently splayed.

sections.title: Special Provisions for addition etc. over existing buildings
paragraphs: (1) Extension of first/second floor permitted to buildings existing on 30th March 2000 even if they don't satisfy regular rules.
(5) Requires average 60 cms open space from all boundaries.

sections.title: Extension in the ground floor
paragraphs: (1) Extension in ground floor permitted if proposed extension satisfies rules.

sections.title: Essentially of permit
paragraphs: (1) No new well shall be dug without permission.
(3) Site plan must show details within 7.5 metres radius.

sections.title: Set back
paragraphs: (2) 1.50 metres set back from other boundaries.
(4) Leech pit/septic tank must be 7.5 metres away from well.

chapter-17.title: Safety Provisions for High Rise Buildings

sections.title: High rise building
paragraphs: Definition: Building having more than four floors and/or 15 metres height.

sections.title: Staircase
paragraphs: (1) At least two staircases required.
(2) Handrail height min 90 cms; baluster gaps max 10 cms.

sections.title: Guard rails or parapets
paragraphs: Height not less than 1.20 metres for slabs/balconies overlooking 2m+ drops.

sections.title: Fire escape stairway
paragraphs: (1) Every high rise building shall have a fire escape stairway.
(4) Straight flight not less than 75 cm wide.

sections.title: Open space for fire fighting
paragraphs: Minimum 5 metres wide open space on one side for fire fighting access.

sections.title: Prohibition for commencing work without permission
paragraphs: No person shall construct or reconstruct a hut without a permit.

sections.title: Provisions regarding distance from road
paragraphs: (1) Minimum 1.00 metre from street (other than NH/SH/District roads).
(2) Minimum 60 cms from other boundaries.

sections.title: Essentiality of permit
paragraphs: Separate permit required for towers, masts, or pole structures.

sections.title: Distance form road and boundaries
paragraphs: (1) Base of tower: Minimum 3 metres from street boundary.
(3) 1.20 metres distance from other plot boundaries.

sections.title: Protective Wall
paragraphs: (1) Protective wall/grill required at 1 metre distance from base.

sections.title: Warning lights and colour specifications
paragraphs: (1) Two Aviation Warning Lights (AWL) required.
(2) Painted with international orange and white bands.

chapter-20.title: Regularisation of Unpermitted Constructions and Deviations

sections.title: Power of the Secretary to regularise certain constructions
paragraphs: Secretary has power to regularise constructions commenced/completed without permit if not in violation of Act/Rules.

sections.title: Submission of application
paragraphs: Procedure for regularization is similar to that for a new permit.

sections.title: Decision and compounding fee
paragraphs: (4) Compounding fee shall be double the amount of permit fee in force.

chapter-21.title: Registration of Architects, Engineers Town Planners, etc.

sections.title: Plans and drawings signees
paragraphs: Plans must be prepared and signed by registered Architects/Engineers/Town Planners.

sections.title: Application and fees
paragraphs: Registration is valid for three years.
Fees: Architect (1000), Engineer A (1000), Supervisor A (500), etc.

sections.title: Constitution of the Commission
paragraphs: Government may constitute an Art and Heritage Commission for the State.

sections.title: Functions of the Commission
paragraphs: Functions include identifying heritage areas and advising on architectural features.

chapter-23.title: Vigilance, Dangerous and Defective Works, Appeal, etc.

sections.title: Secretary to submit report
paragraphs: Secretary must submit quarterly reports to Government on permits and regularization.

sections.title: Vigilance Squad
paragraphs: Vigilance squad in every District to detect and report illegal constructions.

sections.title: Penalty for unlawful building
paragraphs: (1) Fine up to ten thousand rupees for building without permission.

sections.title: Appeal
paragraphs: (1) Appeal may be filed to the Tribunal for Local Self Government Institutions.
